controls.title: Integrity and Ethical Values
controls.objective: Demonstrate commitment to integrity and ethical values.
controls.description: The entity establishes expectations for ethical conduct, communicates them across the organization, and addresses deviations in a timely manner.
controls.guidance: Maintain a code of conduct, communicate expected behavior, train personnel, and document how misconduct is reported and remediated.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm that ethical expectations are documented, acknowledged by personnel, and supported by a repeatable issue handling process.

controls.title: Board Independence and Oversight
controls.objective: Establish independent oversight of internal control.
controls.description: The board or equivalent governing body provides oversight of management and the system of internal control.
controls.guidance: Document governance responsibilities, meeting cadence, escalation paths, and evidence of oversight activities.
controls.aiGuidance: Look for evidence that leadership receives meaningful risk and control information and takes action when needed.

controls.title: Organizational Structure, Authority, and Responsibility
controls.objective: Define structures, reporting lines, authority, and responsibilities.
controls.description: The entity establishes organizational roles and reporting lines that support achievement of control objectives.
controls.guidance: Keep organizational charts, role descriptions, ownership matrices, and approval responsibilities current.
controls.aiGuidance: Validate that accountability for security, compliance, operations, and risk decisions is clearly assigned.

controls.title: Commitment to Competence
controls.objective: Attract, develop, and retain competent personnel.
controls.description: The entity defines required competencies and supports personnel with hiring, training, and performance processes.
controls.guidance: Document role qualifications, onboarding, training, performance review, and continuing education practices.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm that personnel responsible for controls have appropriate qualifications and receive relevant training.

controls.title: Accountability
controls.objective: Hold individuals accountable for internal control responsibilities.
controls.description: The entity establishes accountability through performance expectations, incentives, corrective action, and oversight.
controls.guidance: Tie control responsibilities to roles, performance management, and corrective action processes.
controls.aiGuidance: Check whether control owners are named and whether failures result in documented follow-up.

controls.title: Relevant and Quality Information
controls.objective: Obtain or generate relevant, quality information to support internal control.
controls.description: The entity identifies information requirements and uses reliable information to operate and monitor controls.
controls.guidance: Define reporting needs, data sources, quality expectations, and review procedures for control information.
controls.aiGuidance: Ensure control decisions are based on complete, accurate, and timely information.

controls.title: Internal Communication
controls.objective: Internally communicate information needed to support internal control.
controls.description: The entity communicates objectives, responsibilities, and control information internally.
controls.guidance: Use documented communication channels for security updates, policy changes, incidents, and control responsibilities.
controls.aiGuidance: Verify that employees receive relevant security and compliance information when responsibilities or risks change.

controls.title: External Communication
controls.objective: Communicate relevant information externally.
controls.description: The entity communicates with external parties about matters affecting controls, commitments, and system requirements.
controls.guidance: Maintain customer, vendor, auditor, and regulator communication procedures, including escalation and response ownership.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm external communication responsibilities are assigned and supported by approved messaging paths.

controls.title: Specified Objectives
controls.objective: Specify objectives clearly enough to identify and assess risks.
controls.description: The entity defines objectives for operations, reporting, and compliance that allow risk identification and control design.
controls.guidance: Document business, security, availability, confidentiality, processing integrity, and privacy objectives where applicable.
controls.aiGuidance: Use defined objectives as the basis for deciding which risks and controls are relevant.

controls.title: Risk Identification and Analysis
controls.objective: Identify and analyze risks to achieving objectives.
controls.description: The entity identifies internal and external risks and analyzes their likelihood, impact, and response options.
controls.guidance: Maintain a risk register, scoring method, review cadence, and documented risk treatment decisions.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm risks are assessed consistently and linked to controls or mitigation activities.

controls.title: Fraud Risk Consideration
controls.objective: Consider the potential for fraud when assessing risks.
controls.description: The entity considers fraudulent activity, incentives, pressures, opportunities, and attitudes when assessing risk.
controls.guidance: Include fraud scenarios in risk assessment, access reviews, financial process controls, and incident response planning.
controls.aiGuidance: Look for risks involving privilege misuse, unauthorized changes, and management override.

controls.title: Significant Change Assessment
controls.objective: Identify and assess changes that could significantly affect internal control.
controls.description: The entity evaluates organizational, operational, technology, vendor, and regulatory changes for control impact.
controls.guidance: Connect change management, vendor changes, infrastructure changes, and business changes to risk reassessment.
controls.aiGuidance: Flag major changes that occurred without documented risk and control impact review.

controls.title: Ongoing and Separate Evaluations
controls.objective: Perform evaluations to determine whether controls are present and functioning.
controls.description: The entity uses ongoing monitoring, separate evaluations, or both to assess control design and operation.
controls.guidance: Define monitoring activities, internal reviews, compliance testing, and management review cadence.
requiredEvidence: Internal audit or compliance testing records
controls.aiGuidance: Verify that controls are monitored periodically and exceptions are tracked to closure.

controls.title: Deficiency Evaluation and Communication
controls.objective: Evaluate and communicate control deficiencies.
controls.description: The entity evaluates identified deficiencies and communicates them to parties responsible for corrective action.
controls.guidance: Track control exceptions, assign remediation owners, set target dates, and report significant deficiencies to leadership.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm deficiencies have severity, ownership, remediation deadlines, and completion evidence.

controls.title: Selection and Development of Control Activities
controls.objective: Select and develop control activities that mitigate risks.
controls.description: The entity designs control activities to address risks to objectives at appropriate levels of the organization.
controls.guidance: Map risks to controls, define control frequency, ownership, evidence, and review expectations.
controls.aiGuidance: Check that controls are tied to specific risks and have clear operating criteria.

controls.title: General Technology Controls
controls.objective: Select and develop general controls over technology.
controls.description: The entity designs technology controls over infrastructure, security management, technology acquisition, development, and maintenance.
controls.guidance: Document technology control domains, including access, change management, operations, monitoring, backup, and incident response.
controls.aiGuidance: Prioritize evidence for access, change, vulnerability, logging, and backup controls.

controls.title: Policies and Procedures
controls.objective: Deploy control activities through policies and procedures.
controls.description: The entity establishes policies and procedures that put control activities into operation.
controls.guidance: Maintain approved policies, procedures, review dates, ownership, and employee communication evidence.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm policies are approved, current, communicated, and aligned to actual control activities.

controls.title: Logical Access Security
controls.objective: Implement logical access controls to protect systems and data.
controls.description: The entity uses security software, infrastructure, and architectures to restrict logical access to authorized users.
controls.guidance: Use centralized identity, MFA, role-based access, least privilege, and periodic access reviews.
controls.aiGuidance: Focus on privileged access, MFA enforcement, and evidence that access is limited by role.

controls.title: User Registration and Authorization
controls.objective: Register and authorize users and devices before granting access.
controls.description: The entity authorizes users, credentials, and devices before granting system access.
controls.guidance: Require documented approval for new users, privileged users, service accounts, and registered devices.
controls.aiGuidance: Check whether access was approved before activation and whether approvals match job responsibilities.

controls.title: Access Modification and Removal
controls.objective: Authorize, modify, and remove access based on roles and responsibilities.
controls.description: The entity manages access changes and removals when responsibilities change or users leave.
controls.guidance: Connect HR events, role changes, access requests, terminations, and periodic reviews to access updates.
controls.aiGuidance: Prioritize terminated users, privileged accounts, and dormant accounts for evidence review.

controls.title: Physical Access Restrictions
controls.objective: Restrict physical access to facilities and protected assets.
controls.description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities, infrastructure, and protected information assets.
controls.guidance: Use badge access, visitor controls, locked areas, monitoring, and periodic physical access reviews where applicable.
requiredEvidence: Data center or office access records
controls.aiGuidance: For cloud-native teams, confirm reliance on cloud provider physical security reports and office controls.

controls.title: Protection of Information Assets
controls.objective: Protect information assets through their lifecycle.
controls.description: The entity protects information assets against unauthorized access, disclosure, modification, or destruction.
controls.guidance: Maintain asset handling, encryption, secure storage, disposal, and media sanitization procedures.
controls.aiGuidance: Look for controls over sensitive data storage, transfer, retention, and secure disposal.

controls.title: Boundary Protection
controls.objective: Protect against unauthorized access from outside system boundaries.
controls.description: The entity implements controls to detect, prevent, and restrict unauthorized external access.
controls.guidance: Use network segmentation, firewall rules, secure remote access, endpoint protection, and vulnerability management.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm public exposure is minimized and externally reachable services are monitored and hardened.

controls.title: Data Transmission and Movement
controls.objective: Restrict transmission, movement, and removal of information.
controls.description: The entity restricts data transfer to authorized users, processes, systems, and locations.
controls.guidance: Use encryption, approved transfer methods, data loss prevention, and access controls over exports and removable media.
controls.aiGuidance: Review how sensitive data leaves systems and whether transfer paths are encrypted and authorized.

controls.title: Malicious Software Protection
controls.objective: Prevent, detect, and respond to malicious software.
controls.description: The entity uses controls to prevent or detect unauthorized or malicious software and respond appropriately.
controls.guidance: Deploy endpoint protection, malware scanning, patching, alerting, and response procedures.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm endpoint and server protection is active, monitored, and tied to incident response.

controls.title: Vulnerability Detection and Monitoring
controls.objective: Detect changes and vulnerabilities that could affect security objectives.
controls.description: The entity uses monitoring procedures to identify configuration changes and newly discovered vulnerabilities.
controls.guidance: Run vulnerability scans, monitor configuration drift, assess findings, and track remediation.
controls.aiGuidance: Prioritize unresolved critical vulnerabilities and missing evidence of remediation.

controls.title: Security Event Monitoring
controls.objective: Monitor system components for anomalies and security events.
controls.description: The entity monitors systems for anomalies that may indicate malicious activity, errors, or operational issues.
controls.guidance: Centralize logs, define alerts, review alerts, and document escalation procedures.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm critical systems produce logs and that alerts are reviewed by responsible personnel.

controls.title: Security Event Evaluation
controls.objective: Evaluate security events to determine impact.
controls.description: The entity evaluates detected events to determine whether they represent incidents, control failures, or other issues.
controls.guidance: Define triage criteria, severity levels, investigation steps, and documentation requirements.
controls.aiGuidance: Look for documented event triage and evidence that significant events were investigated.

controls.title: Incident Response
controls.objective: Respond to identified security incidents.
controls.description: The entity executes incident response procedures for identified security incidents.
controls.guidance: Maintain an incident response plan, roles, escalation procedures, communication steps, and tabletop exercises.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm incidents have ownership, timeline, root cause, containment, remediation, and closure evidence.

controls.title: Incident Recovery
controls.objective: Recover from identified security incidents.
controls.description: The entity identifies, develops, and implements recovery activities after security incidents.
controls.guidance: Document recovery procedures, restoration steps, post-incident reviews, and control improvements.
controls.aiGuidance: Check whether incidents result in lessons learned and control improvements.

controls.title: Change Management
controls.objective: Authorize, test, approve, and implement changes.
controls.description: The entity manages system changes through authorization, design, development, testing, approval, and implementation controls.
controls.guidance: Require change tickets, approvals, testing evidence, deployment records, rollback plans, and emergency change review.
controls.aiGuidance: Review whether production changes are approved, tested, traceable, and separated from direct unauthorized deployment.

controls.title: Business Disruption Risk Mitigation
controls.objective: Identify and mitigate risks from business disruptions.
controls.description: The entity identifies and develops mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.
controls.guidance: Maintain business continuity, disaster recovery, backup, availability monitoring, and resilience procedures.
controls.aiGuidance: Confirm continuity plans are tested and aligned with service commitments.

controls.title: Vendor and Business Partner Risk Mitigation
controls.objective: Assess and manage risks associated with vendors and business partners.
controls.description: The entity assesses third-party risks and manages vendor relationships that could affect system objectives.
controls.guidance: Maintain vendor inventory, due diligence, security reviews, contracts, monitoring, and periodic reassessments.
controls.aiGuidance: Prioritize vendors with access to customer data, production systems, or critical services.
